payload.text: EduManage Abstract: The Institute Management System is an all-in-one platform designed to streamline the management of educational institutions. It includes features for managing trainers, students, attendance, batch allocation, and task assignments. This system provides an admin interface for managing users and operations, while trainers can allocate tasks to students, track their attendance, and manage their batches. The project is developed using Spring Boot for backend services and Hibernate for database management, ensuring a scalable and efficient platform. Objective: 1. Admin Login and User Management: o Provide secure login for administrators to manage users, view reports, and monitor system activities. o Admins can create, modify, and delete trainer, student, and batch records. 2. Trainer Dashboard: o Trainers will have their own dashboards to view their assigned batches, track student attendance, and allocate tasks. o Trainers can mark attendance and assign tasks to students based on the course structure. 3. Student Attendance Management: o Record and track attendance of students for each batch session. o Generate reports on student attendance, with options for monthly, weekly, or individual session tracking. 4. Batch Allocation: o Admins can allocate students to different batches based on their course selection, availability, and trainer assignments. o View all batch details, including the trainer, scheduled timings, and student roster. 5. Task Assignment: o Trainers can assign specific tasks (such as assignments, projects, or exams) to students within their batches. o Students can submit their tasks, and trainers can grade them within the system. Operating System Requirements:   Server: Linux (Ubuntu, CentOS) or Windows Server.   Client: Windows 10/11 or macOS for accessing the system through a web interface. Hardware Requirements:   Server: o Processor: Quad-core or higher (Intel i5/i7 or AMD Ryzen). o RAM: Minimum 8 GB. o Storage: SSD with 100 GB or more. o Network: Stable internet connection (1 Gbps recommended).   Client: o Processor: Dual-core or higher. o RAM: Minimum 4 GB. o Storage: 500 MB of free space. Software Requirements:   Backend: o Spring Boot 3.0 or higher. o Hibernate ORM 6.0 or higher. o JDK 17 or later.   Frontend (Optional): o Angular/React for building the user interface.   Database: o PostgreSQL/MySQL for storing data related to students, trainers, attendance, and tasks.   Tools: o IDE: IntelliJ IDEA or Eclipse. o Build Tool: Maven or Gradle. o API Documentation: Swagger/OpenAPI. o Testing: JUnit, Mockito. Modules: 1. Admin Module: o Admin login, dashboard, user management (trainer, student, batch). o Overview of attendance, tasks, and batches. 2. Trainer Module: o Trainer login, dashboard with batch and student details. o Attendance tracking, task allocation, and grading. 3. Student Module: o Student login, viewing assigned batches, attendance status, and assigned tasks. o Task submission and feedback system. 4. Batch Allocation Module: o Admin assigns students to batches, manages batch schedules and trainer assignments. o Trainers and students can view batch details. 5. Attendance and Task Management Module: o Real-time attendance recording for students. o Task assignment and grading system. Conclusion: The Institute Management System is designed to automate and simplify the management of educational institutions, improving efficiency in student-trainer interactions. By leveraging Spring Boot and Hibernate, this platform offers a secure, scalable, and user-friendly solution for managing trainers, students, batches, attendance, and task allocation. It aims to reduce administrative overhead, enhance transparency, and provide both trainers and students with a seamless experience in managing tasks and attendance. The system is highly customizable, allowing future integrations and enhancements

**Defining the React Application**

I'm now zeroing in on the core goal: building the "EduManage" React application, an Institute Management System. I'm taking the abstract and requirements seriously, using them to guide my understanding of the project's purpose and scope. This first stage is setting a firm foundation for the development.


**Detailing Core Components**

I'm now diving into the specifics. I'm focusing on React 18+, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and Gemini API integration, although the prompt is less concerned with those details. I'm carefully outlining the core modules and their features: Admin, Trainer, and Student, understanding their individual functionalities as the base of the Institute Management System. The UI/UX should use "world-class" aesthetics, responsiveness, and cleanliness.


**Outlining App Structure**

I'm solidifying the file structure now, mapping out how the pieces will fit together. I'm focusing on key files like `index.tsx`, `App.tsx`, and shared types. The use of a simple `metadata.json` for a name and description seems sensible for starting, and I plan to include mock data in `constants.ts` to cover the backend gap. Layout and StatCards will sit in components, while the AI service lives in the `services` directory. The view structure is still taking shape, too.


**Defining Data Structures**

I'm now focusing on core data structures and mock data. I've defined a `Role` enum and interfaces for `User`, `Batch`, `Task`, and `AttendanceRecord` within `types.ts`. I am writing detailed mock data in `constants.ts` for users, batches, tasks, and attendance, to make the UI populated. I am aiming for realistic data. I want to build a rich user experience from the beginning.

\n\n


**Mapping App Views**

I'm now making solid progress on the UI's structure, focusing on the core views. I'm building out admin, trainer, and student dashboards, including mock data to populate components early on. I'm using `currentUser` and `currentView` for state management, as a simpler router replacement. Gemini integration is coming along. The "Get AI Insight" button will be added to the Admin dashboard.

I am also making great progress on the UI/UX front. I'm going with a professional blue and indigo color scheme with gray backgrounds and white card elements. I am using Tailwind for all styling, meeting the no external CSS requirement.


**Refining Features and Implementation**

I'm now zeroing in on specific features and how to code them efficiently. I'm leaning toward a simple login screen with role selection via a button click for demonstration. State will be managed in `App` and passed down, or constants will be directly imported for read-only data. The "Smart Assistant" modal, powered by the Gemini API, is also planned. I've chosen `lucide-react` for icons and `recharts` for charts. I'm balancing feature scope with the goal of a concise output.


**Simulating User Interactions**

I'm now focusing on simulating the database interactions within the frontend. Since a persistent database isn't possible here, I'll use `useState` to manage user, batch, and task data in memory. For the "Batch Allocation" feature, I'll implement a straightforward "Select Student -> Add to Batch" interface in the Admin view, as drag-and-drop is too complex. I'll maintain the core data structures with `useState` hooks at the top level of `App.tsx`.

payload.text:
Design a modern, professional, and responsive authentication system for an Institute Management System named "EduManage".

The system should include:

1. LOGIN PAGE
- Clean, minimal UI with institute branding
- Login using Email + Password
- Email-based role detection:
  - Emails ending with @admin.edumanage.com → Admin login
  - Emails ending with @trainer.edumanage.com → Trainer login
  - All other verified emails → Student login
- Show role badge dynamically after email input (Admin / Trainer / Student)
- Remember Me checkbox
- Forgot Password link
- Secure and professional look suitable for educational institutions

2. REGISTRATION (SIGN UP) PAGE
- Allow signup ONLY using email
- Auto role assignment based on email domain:
  - Admin email domain → Admin account
  - Trainer email domain → Trainer account
  - Normal email → Student account
- Registration form fields:
  - Full Name
  - Email Address (domain-based validation)
  - Password
  - Confirm Password
  - Phone Number
  - For Students: Course selection dropdown
  - For Trainers: Expertise / Subject selection
- Show info message:
  "Your role will be automatically assigned based on your email domain"
- Disable manual role selection (for security)

3. UI / UX REQUIREMENTS
- Modern card-based layout
- Soft shadows, rounded corners
- Professional color palette (blue / indigo / white)
- Mobile-first responsive design
- Subtle animations for form transitions
- Input validation and inline error messages
- Password strength indicator

4. ROLE-BASED REDIRECTION (UI ONLY)
- After login/signup:
  - Admin → Admin Dashboard
  - Trainer → Trainer Dashboard
  - Student → Student Dashboard

5. EXTRA POLISH
- Add institute logo placeholder
- Add footer with:
  "© EduManage – Institute Management System"
- Use accessibility-friendly labels and contrast
- Production-ready UI suitable for React or Next.js

Generate only UI components and layouts (no backend code).

To implement this comprehensive authentication system for "EduManage", I will update `App.tsx` to include the new Login and Registration views with the specified logic and design.

I will:
1.  **Create an `AuthLayout` component** to share the branding and structure between Login and Signup.
2.  **Implement `LoginPage`** with dynamic role detection based on email input.
3.  **Implement `SignupPage`** with fields adapting to the detected role (Student vs. Trainer).
4.  **Update the main `App` component** to manage the authentication state (view switching between Login/Signup) and integrate with the existing dashboard logic.
5.  **Remove the old `LoginScreen`** and replace it with this new system.

Here are the changes:

Design a modern, professional Admin Dashboard UI for an Institute Management System called "EduManage".

ADMIN DASHBOARD FEATURES:

1. DASHBOARD OVERVIEW
- Clean top navigation bar with:
  - Institute logo
  - Admin profile dropdown
  - Notifications icon
- Overview cards:
  - Total Students
  - Total Trainers
  - Total Batches
  - Today's Attendance %
- Use icons, soft shadows, and professional colors

2. USER MANAGEMENT
- Tabs or sidebar sections:
  - Manage Students
  - Manage Trainers
- Student Management:
  - Add / Edit / Delete students
  - Assign students to batches
  - View attendance summary
- Trainer Management:
  - Add / Edit / Delete trainers
  - Assign trainers to batches
  - View trainer performance summary

3. BATCH MANAGEMENT
- Create and manage batches
- Batch details:
  - Batch name
  - Course
  - Trainer assigned
  - Schedule (time & days)
  - Student list
- Batch status (Active / Completed)

4. ATTENDANCE & TASK OVERVIEW
- Attendance reports:
  - Batch-wise
  - Monthly summary
- Task overview:
  - Tasks assigned
  - Submission status
- Export options (UI only): PDF / Excel buttons

5. UI / UX REQUIREMENTS
- Sidebar navigation (Dashboard, Users, Batches, Attendance, Tasks, Reports)
- Card-based layout
- Responsive design (desktop & tablet)
- Professional blue / indigo color palette
- Smooth hover effects and transitions
- Table pagination & search UI

6. ROLE & SECURITY UI
- Show "Admin Access" badge
- Disable restricted actions visually
- Confirmation modal for delete actions

**Considering Dark Mode Implementation**

I'm building a `ThemeToggle` component, a sun/moon button, to inject into `AuthLayout` and `Layout`. I will refactor existing components using Tailwind's `dark:` modifiers for colors on backgrounds, text, and borders, with special focus on inputs and UI elements.


**Refactoring Theme Toggle**

I've integrated `Moon` and `Sun` icons from `lucide-react` directly into the toggle component, using state to control which icon is displayed. I've updated `AuthLayout` to accept and render this toggle in the top-right corner. The `dark:` classes are added in the container and the text.

I'm now diving into updating various views. I need to customize recharts and table styles, as well as forms, modals, and backgrounds. My styling strategy is taking shape. I'm focusing on backgrounds, text, and borders, specifying how they change in dark mode. Specifically, in `App.tsx`, I'm updating `AuthLayout` and other components like `LoginPage` to follow the styling guidelines.


**Addressing Recharts Styling**

I am now focusing on integrating dark mode with Recharts. I realized Tailwind doesn't automatically affect Recharts components. I'm considering several approaches for chart tooltips, either styling the tooltip wrapper explicitly, hardcoding dark-friendly styles, using CSS variables, or setting a container style, to avoid prop drilling theme state. The table, admin dashboard, layout, and various views are all receiving dark mode styling updates.


**Styling Recharts Tooltips**

I'm now focusing on Recharts tooltips. I've found `contentStyle` in the `Tooltip` component to be useful, as I can set a dark-friendly background. I'm passing the theme state from `App.tsx` down to components like `AdminDashboard`. In `index.html`, I've added `darkMode: 'class'` to Tailwind config and `dark:bg-gray-900 dark:text-gray-100` to the body. In `App.tsx`, I've imported `Sun` and `Moon`, defined `ThemeToggle`, and updated the `App` component to manage `isDarkMode`. I'm passing `themeProps` to layouts and adding `dark:` classes everywhere. I'm updating components with dark-mode considerations, like the dynamic `RoleBadge` component, updating its config object to use dark mode classes.


**Refining Styling Details**

I'm now refining the dark mode styles across several UI elements. For charts in the admin dashboard, I've decided on neutral grey grid lines to avoid prop drilling. I have updated the application to ensure it does not "refresh" (lose state) unnecessarily.

**Changes made:**

1.  **State Persistence (`App.tsx`)**:
    *   **Login State**: The application now remembers if you were on the "Sign In" or "Sign Up" screen using `localStorage`. If you refresh the browser, you will stay on the same screen.
    *   **Theme Persistence**: The Dark/Light mode preference is saved to `localStorage`. It persists across refreshes.

2.  **Prevent Unwanted Submissions (`App.tsx`)**:
    *   Added `type="button"` to the **Theme Toggle** button. This ensures that clicking the moon/sun icon never accidentally submits a form it might be sitting near.
    *   Ensured the **Back to Sign In/Sign Up** buttons have `type="button"`.
    *   Confirmed `e.preventDefault()` is present in the `handleSubmit` function to stop the standard browser page reload on form submission.

3.  **Admin Email Logic**: Retained the logic where selecting "Administrator" automatically fills the email `sakshipawar5840@gmail.com`.
